body.title: CLOSED: Extend watchlist — per-instance instance.watch_problems DB tree
body.result: instance.watch_problems table + full API + lever_watcher + escalate.py auto-deposit; 9 tests
body.description:
The watch list has two tiers: clan.memories WATCH_Q_*/WATCH_T_* (already exists — shared biological/interest/question nodes across all instances) and a per-instance unsolved-problem tree (new). This ticket builds the second tier.

Create instance.watch_problems table in the instance.* schema (alongside ring_memory, twm_observations):

  CREATE TABLE instance.watch_problems (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES instance.watch_problems(id),
    problem TEXT NOT NULL,
    lever_description TEXT,
    watch_condition TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    last_surfaced_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}'
  );

API in new wild_igor/igor/cognition/watch_problems.py:
  add_watch_problem(problem, lever_description=None, watch_condition=None, parent_id=None) -> int
  read_active_problems() -> list[dict]
  resolve_problem(id: int) -> None
  mark_surfaced(id: int) -> None

Wire into escalate.py: every call to _escalate_to_channel() also calls add_watch_problem() so escalation leaves a durable per-instance trace.

The clan-level clan.memories WATCH_Q_*/WATCH_T_* nodes are the "universal watching" (interests, questions, drives). instance.watch_problems is the "right now I'm stuck on this" watching. Both are DB trees.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/watch_problems.py (NEW), wild_igor/igor/cognition/escalate.py (call add_watch_problem as side effect), migration in wild_igor/igor/migrations/ or equivalent
**Design rules:** instance.* schema for per-instance data. Tree via parent_id.
**Scope boundary:** In scope: table creation + API + wire into escalate.py. Out of scope: lever watcher (T-igor-lever-watcher), clan.memories WATCH_Q_*/WATCH_T_* (don't touch), watchlist.md error log (untouched — separate concern).
**Test plan:** Test add_watch_problem inserts row; test parent_id forms tree; test read_active_problems excludes resolved; test mark_surfaced updates last_surfaced_at; test escalate.py side-effect creates watch entry.
